title: MLCB KG Extraction Schema
description: Instructions for extracting entities and relationships from MLCB lecture transcripts

pass_1_source_inventory.purpose: List all Raw/Sources/ files, confirm metadata, record lecture-to-file mapping.

pass_2_structural_extraction.purpose: Extract lecture structure: sections, subsections, topics per lecture.

pass_3_entity_extraction.purpose: Identify all entities per lecture. Classify by type from entity_ontology.yaml.
rules: Bold or title-cased terms are extraction candidates.
Named algorithms, models, datasets, and tools are always extracted.
Biological terms in bold or defined in context are extracted as biological_entity.
Assign preliminary id in kebab-case from label.

pass_4_relationship_extraction.purpose: Identify relationships between extracted entities using context.
rules: Use only relationship types from relationship_ontology.yaml.
Record source file path as evidence for each edge.
Assign weight 0.9 for explicit statements, 0.7 for implied.

pass_5_canonicalization.purpose: Merge duplicate nodes. Unify aliases. Normalize ids.
rules: If two nodes share a label or alias, merge into one with combined sources.
Canonical id is the most common or most descriptive name in kebab-case.
Record all alternate names in aliases field.

pass_6_scoring.purpose: Compute importance scores for nodes and confidence for edges.
pass_6_scoring.output: Scored fields on nodes and edges

pass_7_wiki_update.purpose: Ensure every high-importance node has a compiled Wiki page.
rules: Nodes with importance >= 0.6 must have wiki_page.
Create stub pages for missing high-importance entities.
Add KG Extraction section to each lecture file.

pass_8_retrieval_artifact_generation.purpose: Serialize final KG into retrieval-ready formats.

pass_9_validation.purpose: Check referential integrity, orphan nodes, missing sources.
checks: Every edge source and target must exist as a node id.
Every node must have at least one source.
No two nodes share an id.
All aliases are unique across the alias map.

entity_extraction_prompts.identify_entities: From this lecture text, list all named entities. For each: id (kebab-case), label, type (from ontology), 1-sentence description, and the sentence from the text that supports it.
entity_extraction_prompts.classify_type: Given this entity label and context sentence, assign exactly one type: concept, method, model, biological_entity, data_type, application, tool, lecture, topic, person, equation.
entity_extraction_prompts.extract_relationships: For each pair of entities in this excerpt, identify if a relationship from the relationship ontology applies. Output: source_id, relation, target_id, supporting_sentence.
